one_time_description: Emergency Measures expenditures related to COVID-19 and severe weather events
economic_notes: Nation is developing alternate sources of revenue including VLT/gaming, tobacco tax, bulk fuel, smoke shop, and economic development corporation. Multiplex project under construction with $3.2M Peace Hills Trust loan approved and $1.196M First Peoples Economic Growth Fund approved. Nation is a sole shareholder of Sandy Bay Economic Development Corporation. Business plan development with 7475323 MB Ltd.
notes: Audit is qualified due to incomplete accounting records for Sandy Bay Indigenous Skills And Employment Training Program (ISET) - auditors could not confirm completeness of accounts payable and deferred revenue. Fiscal year is 2021 despite auditor report dated March 7, 2024. 12 entities fully consolidated. No schedule of remuneration provided. Large deferred revenue balance of $9.85M including significant pandemic-related ISC funding. CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative restricted cash of $4.25M. Potential contingent liabilities: $343,876 smoke shop contractor claim, $250,000 union grievance, and up to $350,000 ISC audit recovery for 2013/14-2014/15. First Peoples Economic Growth Fund $250,000 non-repayable contribution recorded as deferred government assistance.
